title: Chains — Load (Read-Only)
deliverable: One tap on a chain card appends all its steps to the current queue and returns to main view; 7 default chains seeded from an in-memory constant; `hooks/useChains.js` read-side; `components/Chains.jsx` list view wired into the view-router.

in: Chain data shape definition: id, name, emoji, steps[], position, is_default
DEFAULT_CHAINS in-memory seed constant with all 7 chains and their exact Turkish steps
hooks/useChains.js — read-only hook: exposes chains list and loadChain(id) action
loadChain appends all steps of the chosen chain to the queue (via useQueue from Block 9) then navigates to 'main'
components/Chains.jsx — full-screen chain list view reached via view-router 'chains' state (Block 9 ships the router skeleton)
Back button in Chains.jsx navigating to 'main'
Chain card UI: emoji + name, one card per chain, tap to load
Empty state copy if chains list is somehow empty
out: Merge-on-login seeding of default chains for authed users (Block 18)
Any form, textarea, or input in Chains.jsx

files.purpose: Exports DEFAULT_CHAINS array — the authoritative in-memory seed constant for all 7 default chains with exact Turkish steps, positions, and is_default flags. Single source of truth for seed data used by useChains and later by Block 14 persistence seeding.

React hook. Exposes: chains (array, derived from DEFAULT_CHAINS for this block), loadChain(chainId) function that calls appendSteps from useQueue then navigates to main. No write operations in this block.
Chains list view. Full-screen. Renders a scrollable list of chain cards (emoji + name). Tap a card calls loadChain. Back button sets view to 'main'. Emerald/gold theme consistent with app.
Add 'chains' case to the view-router switch/conditional so <Chains /> is rendered with the correct props (setView, appendSteps or the useQueue reference).

implementation_steps.details: Export a frozen array of 7 chain objects. Each object has: id (fixed hardcoded UUID — same values reused verbatim by Blocks 13, 14, and 18), name (Turkish string), emoji (single emoji string), steps (array of Turkish step strings in order), position (0-indexed int), is_default (true). Use the exact step text from PRD §3.3. IDs (use these exact UUID values, no substitution): Namaz Hazırlığı → 'b89e0940-9583-4803-9210-d73924e6cfa4', Odak Bloğu → '16064f10-a06a-4945-8340-5d0277ebe288', Sabah Demiri → '17d995ea-0b2f-4110-b3c5-8ff06778a4bb', Abdest → 'f905b229-be82-43c5-a267-6f9c38d5c038', Uyku Hazırlığı → 'c1ba5c76-b02b-4f5b-bd85-cfe11ab91b1f', Evi Topla → '3a519d0d-f060-4f1b-be5f-efec4325610a', Güne Başlama → '72990e01-d127-410e-88cd-f0c2e3b9edc0'. These UUIDs are locked (decision C1) and must not be regenerated. Freeze the array with Object.freeze to prevent accidental mutation.

implementation_steps.description: Verify exact step lists match PRD §3.3 verbatim
implementation_steps.details: Namaz Hazırlığı (🕌, 5 steps): ['banyoya git (abdest için)', 'abdest al', 'odana su getir', 'su iç', 'seccadenin önünde dur']. Odak Bloğu (🔬, 5 steps): ['zamanlayıcıyı aç', '15 dk başlat', 'proje notlarını aç', 'sadece ilk sayfayı oku', 'tek bir iş seç']. Sabah Demiri (☀️, 5 steps): ['yatakta doğrul', 'ayaklarını yere koy', 'ayağa kalk', 'banyoya yürü', 'yüzünü soğuk suyla yıka']. Abdest (💧, 7 steps): ['elleri yıka', 'ağza su ver', 'buruna su ver', 'yüzü yıka', 'kolları yıka', 'başı mesh et', 'ayakları yıka']. Uyku Hazırlığı (🌙, 4 steps): ['telefonu bırak', 'diş fırçala', 'ışıkları kapat', 'yatağa gir']. Evi Topla (🧹, 4 steps): ['bir yüzeyi temizle', 'çöpü topla', 'bulaşığı koy', 'tek bir çeki düzen']. Güne Başlama (☕, 3 steps): ['su iç', 'bugünün tek önceliğini seç', 'ilk küçük adımı at'].

Import DEFAULT_CHAINS from '../data/defaultChains'. Import useQueue from './useQueue' (Block 9) to access appendSteps. Accept setView as a parameter (or receive it via prop drilling from App — see Q1). Expose: { chains, loadChain }. loadChain(chainId): find chain by id in chains, call appendSteps(chain.steps), then call setView('main'). For this block, chains === DEFAULT_CHAINS (no localStorage read yet). appendSteps from useQueue must accept an array of step strings; verify Block 9 exposes this signature or note it as an interface contract.

Props: { setView, appendSteps } — or use useChains hook internally with setView injected. Render: (1) Header bar with a back-chevron button (←) that calls setView('main'), title 'Zincirler' in gold, no other chrome. (2) Scrollable chain list. (3) Each chain card: large emoji left, name right, full-width tap target (min 56px height for ADHD-friendly finger tap). On tap call loadChain(chain.id). Inline styles only, CSS vars for emerald/gold palette consistent with rest of app. Empty state: 'Henüz zincir yok.' (won't appear in practice given seed, but guard it). No edit/delete affordances in this block — those are Block 13.

implementation_steps.description: Wire Chains view into App.jsx view-router
implementation_steps.details: Locate the view-router in App.jsx (the conditional/switch rendering Queue, AddSteps, etc. from Block 9). Add case for view === 'chains': render <Chains setView={setView} appendSteps={appendSteps} /> (or pass the hook reference pattern used by other views). Ensure the 'Zincirler' button/link in the main action bar (already present from Block 9 per PRD §6 — 'Ekle / Zincirler' action bar) calls setView('chains'). If Block 9 did not yet wire the Zincirler button, add it now inside the main action bar in Queue.jsx.

implementation_steps.description: Verify appendSteps interface contract with Block 9
implementation_steps.details: useQueue from Block 9 must expose appendSteps(stepsArray: string[]): void — this appends multiple items to the queue in order without replacing it (PRD §3.3, §7.3 key behaviours). If Block 9 only exposed addStep (singular), extend useQueue here to add appendSteps as a batch variant: iterate stepsArray and push each as a queue item. Each appended step should get a fresh id (crypto.randomUUID() or Date.now()-based fallback) and be placed after the current last item.

implementation_steps.description: Manual smoke test across all 7 chains
implementation_steps.details: For each chain: navigate to Zincirler, tap the chain, confirm view returns to main, confirm all steps appear appended in correct order at the tail of the queue, confirm previously existing queue items are untouched. Specifically test loading a second chain after the first to confirm stacking (append semantics). Test with an empty queue and with a queue that already has items.

data_structures.name: Chain
data_structures.shape: { id: string, name: string, emoji: string, steps: string[], position: number, is_default: boolean }
data_structures.notes: id is a fixed hardcoded UUID (decision C1 — locked). The 7 seed UUIDs are defined once in DEFAULT_CHAINS and reused verbatim by Blocks 13, 14, and 18 — no slug migration, no regeneration. steps is an ordered array of Turkish physical-action strings. position is 0-indexed display order. is_default=true for all seed entries; false for user-created chains (Block 13+).

data_structures.name: DEFAULT_CHAINS
data_structures.notes: In-memory constant in src/data/defaultChains.js. Not mutated at runtime in this block. Each entry carries its fixed UUID id (decision C1). Block 14 uses this as the seed when localStorage key 'niyet_chains' is absent; it must write these same UUIDs verbatim. Block 16/18 uses is_default=true and these same UUIDs to seed the Supabase chains table on first authenticated sign-in.

interfaces.name: useChains
interfaces.signature: useChains(setView: (view: string) => void): { chains: Chain[], loadChain: (chainId: string) => void }
interfaces.purpose: Read-only hook. Returns the current chains list (DEFAULT_CHAINS in this block) and loadChain which appends steps to the queue and navigates to main. setView is passed in so the hook can trigger navigation — avoids needing a context in this block.

interfaces.name: appendSteps
interfaces.purpose: Exported from useQueue (Block 9). Appends each step as a new queue item at the tail, in order, without replacing existing items. This is the core PRD §3.3 / §7.3 'append not replace' contract.

interfaces.name: Chains component props
interfaces.signature: { setView: (view: string) => void, appendSteps: (steps: string[]) => void }
interfaces.purpose: Chains.jsx receives setView for back navigation and appendSteps so useChains can load steps. Alternatively appendSteps can be obtained inside useChains via the useQueue hook — see Q1 for the tradeoff.

acceptance_criteria: Tapping 'Zincirler' from the main action bar navigates to the Chains view
The Chains view lists all 7 default chains in PRD order with correct emoji and Turkish name
Tapping any chain card immediately navigates back to the main queue view
All steps of the tapped chain are appended to the tail of the queue in exact PRD order
If the queue already had items, those items remain untouched at the front; chain steps follow
Loading a second chain appends its steps after the first chain's steps (stacking works)
Loading a chain into an empty queue works — all steps appear as the full queue
Each step string is verbatim Turkish from PRD §3.3 — no truncation, no translation

Namaz Hazırlığı has exactly 5 steps, Abdest has exactly 7 steps, Uyku Hazırlığı has exactly 4 steps, Evi Topla has exactly 4 steps, Güne Başlama has exactly 3 steps, Odak Bloğu has exactly 5 steps, Sabah Demiri has exactly 5 steps
Back button in Chains view returns to main without modifying the queue
No chain is editable or deletable from this view (those controls must not appear)
No packages added to package.json — all functionality uses existing React + DOM APIs
verification: Open app → tap Zincirler button in action bar → Chains view renders with 7 cards
Read the step count badge or render count for each chain against the PRD table
Add 2 steps manually → tap Namaz Hazırlığı → main view shows original 2 steps then 5 Namaz steps in order
From empty queue tap Abdest → queue shows exactly 7 steps starting with 'elleri yıka'
Tap Namaz Hazırlığı then tap Abdest → queue has 5 + 7 = 12 steps in correct order
Tap back button in Chains view → returns to main, queue unchanged
Console has no errors after any chain load
Inspect DEFAULT_CHAINS import in browser devtools — confirm Object.freeze prevents mutation
Verify step text encoding: 'ğ', 'ı', 'ş', 'ç', 'ü', 'ö' characters render correctly (UTF-8 file saved correctly)

questionable_decisions.decision: useChains accepts setView as a parameter rather than reading it from a React context
questionable_decisions.assumption: The app does not yet have a ViewContext or NavigationContext; view state lives in App.jsx and is prop-drilled. This is consistent with Block 9's pattern.
alternatives: Create a ViewContext in Block 9 or here and consume it in useChains
Do not navigate inside the hook — instead return the steps and let Chains.jsx call appendSteps + setView directly, keeping the hook pure
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Make useChains a pure data hook (return chains + getChainById), and let Chains.jsx call appendSteps and setView directly on tap. This is simpler, more testable, and avoids coupling the hook to navigation side-effects. The hook stays reusable for Block 13 (CRUD) and Block 14 (persistence) without modification.

questionable_decisions.decision: Chain id is a Turkish slug string in the seed constant rather than a uuid
questionable_decisions.assumption: Block 14 will introduce localStorage persistence and needs stable, human-readable ids for the seed to deduplicate on first load. Slug ids are easier to read in devtools and logs.
alternatives: Use uuid v4 from the start (requires crypto.randomUUID or a polyfill) — consistent with Supabase schema
Use numeric index as id — simpler but breaks if order changes
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Keep slug ids in the seed constant. Block 14 should treat the slug as the canonical dedup key when seeding localStorage, then assign uuid only when persisting user-created chains. Document the id-type duality clearly in defaultChains.js.
questionable_decisions.resolution: C1 — locked by user: use the fixed hardcoded UUIDs listed in the spec; reused across Blocks 13/14/18.

questionable_decisions.decision: appendSteps is added to useQueue (Block 9) rather than implemented inline in useChains
questionable_decisions.assumption: useQueue is the single owner of queue mutation logic per the PRD architecture (§7.2 hooks/useQueue.js — queue CRUD over the local-first layer). Duplicating append logic in useChains would violate single ownership and diverge in Block 14 when persistence is added.
alternatives: Implement append directly in useChains as a local helper that reads/writes localStorage directly — avoids touching Block 9
Have Chains.jsx call addStep in a loop — simple but not atomic
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Extend useQueue with appendSteps(stepsArray). This is the correct single-ownership approach and will be trivially extended in Block 14. If Block 9 is already closed/reviewed, note this as a minor amendment to Block 9 rather than a new block.

questionable_decisions.decision: DEFAULT_CHAINS is an in-memory constant with no localStorage read in Block 12
questionable_decisions.assumption: Block 14 introduces persistence; until then any user-created chains (Block 13) would be lost on refresh. This is acceptable for this block since Block 13 (create/edit/delete) depends on Block 12 — users cannot create chains before the list view exists, so no data to lose yet.
alternatives: Implement a minimal localStorage read in useChains now, returning DEFAULT_CHAINS as fallback — makes Block 14 smaller
Skip DEFAULT_CHAINS constant entirely and seed directly into localStorage in this block
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Keep DEFAULT_CHAINS as a pure in-memory constant for Block 12. Block 14 adds the localStorage layer using this same constant as the seed. Do not conflate concerns across blocks.

open_questions: Does Block 9 (useQueue) already expose appendSteps(stepsArray) or only addStep(text)? If only addStep, Block 12 must amend Block 9 or add the batch method.
Does Block 9's view-router already render a placeholder for 'chains' view and wire the 'Zincirler' button in the action bar? If not, Block 12 must add both.

Should each appended step carry a chainId reference (matching the done_items.chain_id in the Supabase schema §4.3) so that completions can later be attributed to a chain? This is useful for Block 16/18 but adds complexity now.

What is the exact visual treatment for the chain card — does it show a step count (e.g. '5 adım') as a secondary label, or emoji + name only? PRD does not specify; a step count reduces cognitive load for ADHD users.
Should loading a chain show a brief confirmation ('Zincir yüklendi — 5 adım eklendi') as a toast/snackbar before returning to main? PRD §3.1 empty-state copy suggests visual feedback is valued.

Is there a maximum queue length cap? Loading a long chain (7 steps) onto a queue with many items could produce a long list — PRD does not set a cap but ADHD UX principles suggest this should be invisible ('N adım sırada' count, not a visible wall).
